diagrams.title: AI Hierarchy Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the nested relationship between AI, Machine Learning, and Deep Learning
elements: Examples listed under each: AI - Robotics, NLP; ML - Regression, SVM; DL - CNN, RNN
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the scope and relationships between AI subfields

diagrams.title: Turing Test Setup
diagrams.description: Standard Turing Test configuration showing evaluator, human, and machine
elements: Goal: Evaluator must identify which is human/machine
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how the Turing Test works to measure machine intelligence

diagrams.title: AI Capability Spectrum
elements: Current: ANI (Narrow AI) - Siri, Tesla Autopilot, Deep Blue
Future: AGI (General AI) - Human-level, performs any task
Future+: ASI (Super AI) - Surpasses all human capabilities
Arrow showing progression from current to theoretical
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the evolution and future of AI capabilities

diagrams.title: Symbolic vs Connectionist AI
diagrams.description: Two different approaches to building AI systems
elements: Symbolic AI: Input → Rules/Logic → Knowledge Base → Output (top-down)
Connectionist AI: Input → Neural Network Layers → Hidden Layers → Output (bottom-up)
Symbolic examples: Expert Systems, Logic Programming
Connectionist examples: Neural Networks, Deep Learning

visuals.title: What Is Ai
visuals.description: Visual diagram for what is ai
